title: PwD - Navigate to Catalog Tab from Home Page - Functional Test Plan (33 Test Cases)
overview: Functional testing of SCRUM-73: PwD - Navigate to Catalog Tab from Home Page. Validates that a PwD user or visitor can navigate from the homepage to the Assistive Device Catalog page via the Catalog tab in the global navigation bar. Covers navigation link visibility, redirection, page load performance, page title/heading, catalog header elements, Shopping Tips section, responsive layout, and edge cases. Accessibility testing is explicitly out of scope.

testCases.title: Catalog Tab Visible in Global Navigation Bar
testCases.preconditions: User is on the homepage at desktop viewport.
expectedResults: The global navigation bar is displayed at the top of the page.
A 'Catalog' link is visible in the navigation bar.

testCases.title: Clicking Catalog Redirects to Catalog Landing Page
testCases.preconditions: User is on the homepage with the navigation bar visible.
steps: Click the 'Catalog' link in the navigation bar.
expectedResults: The URL changes to /catalog.
The page displays the 'Assistive Device Catalog' H1 heading.

testCases.title: Catalog Page Loads Within 3 Seconds
testCases.preconditions: User is on the homepage.
steps: Click the 'Catalog' link in the navigation bar.
Measure the time from click to page domcontentloaded event.
expectedResults: The catalog page loads within 3 seconds (domcontentloaded fires within threshold).

testCases.title: Page Title and H1 Heading Display Correctly
testCases.preconditions: User has navigated to the catalog page.
steps: Check the H1 heading on the page.
expectedResults: The browser tab title is 'Assistive Device Catalog – AT/AD Portal'.
The H1 heading displays 'Assistive Device Catalog'.

testCases.title: Expanded Filter Section is Visible
testCases.preconditions: User is on the catalog page at desktop viewport.
steps: Verify the 'Collapse filters' toggle button is visible with aria-expanded='true'.
expectedResults: The filter section is visible and expanded by default.
The 'Collapse filters' toggle button has aria-expanded='true'.

testCases.title: Device Count is Displayed
testCases.preconditions: User is on the catalog page.
expectedResults: A device count text (e.g., 'Showing X devices') is visible on the page.

testCases.title: Search Bar is Visible
testCases.preconditions: User is on the catalog page.
steps: Verify the search bar is visible and enabled.
expectedResults: The search bar is visible and enabled for user input.

testCases.title: All Filter Dropdowns and Controls are Present
testCases.preconditions: User is on the catalog page with filters expanded.
steps: Verify Disability Type, Sub Category, Type, Price Range dropdowns are visible.
Verify Availability checkbox and Min Rating slider are visible.
Verify 'Apply Filters' and 'Reset All' buttons are visible.
expectedResults: Disability Type, Sub Category, Type, and Price Range dropdowns are all visible.
Availability checkbox and Min Rating slider are visible.
'Apply Filters' and 'Reset All' buttons are visible and enabled.

testCases.title: Sorting Dropdown is Present
testCases.preconditions: User is on the catalog page.
steps: Verify the 'Sort by' dropdown is visible.
Verify it contains multiple sorting options.
expectedResults: The 'Sort by' dropdown is visible.
The dropdown contains multiple sorting options.

testCases.title: Pagination is Present
testCases.preconditions: User is on the catalog page with multiple pages of results.
steps: Verify the pagination navigation is visible.
expectedResults: Pagination navigation is visible at the bottom of the catalog listings.
A 'Next' link is visible and clickable.

testCases.title: Shopping Tips & Guidance Section is Visible
testCases.preconditions: User is on the catalog page.
steps: Scroll down to the Shopping Tips section.
Verify 'Shopping Tips & Guidance' heading is visible.
Verify 'Before You Buy' and 'Need Help Choosing?' subsections are visible.
expectedResults: 'Shopping Tips & Guidance' heading is visible.
'Before You Buy' subsection is visible.
'Need Help Choosing?' subsection is visible.

testCases.title: Page Layout Adjusts for Desktop, Tablet, and Mobile
testCases.preconditions: User is on the catalog page.
steps: View at desktop viewport (1280x720) — verify H1, filters, pagination visible.
Resize to tablet viewport (768x1024) — verify key elements visible.
Resize to mobile viewport (375x667) — verify H1 and pagination visible.
Verify no horizontal scrollbar at mobile viewport.
expectedResults: At desktop: H1, filters, and pagination are visible.
At tablet: key elements remain visible and properly laid out.
At mobile: H1 and pagination are visible.
No horizontal scrollbar appears at mobile viewport.

testCases.title: Catalog Tab is Keyboard Activatable
testCases.preconditions: User is on the homepage with keyboard focus available.
steps: Press Enter to activate the link.
Verify navigation to the catalog page.
expectedResults: The 'Catalog' link receives keyboard focus.
Pressing Enter navigates to the catalog page.
The catalog page URL and H1 heading are correct.

testCases.title: Breadcrumb Displays Navigation Path
testCases.preconditions: User is on the catalog page.
steps: Look for a breadcrumb or navigation path element.
expectedResults: A breadcrumb or navigation path element is visible showing the current location.

testCases.title: Mobile Hamburger Menu Contains Catalog Link
testCases.preconditions: User is on the homepage at mobile viewport (375x667).
steps: Navigate to the homepage at mobile viewport.
Tap the hamburger menu to open it.
Verify the 'Catalog' link is visible inside the menu.
Verify navigation to the catalog page.
expectedResults: The hamburger menu opens successfully.
The 'Catalog' link is visible inside the menu.
Tapping 'Catalog' navigates to the catalog page.
The catalog page loads with correct H1 heading.

testCases.title: Catalog Tab Visible from Other Pages
testCases.preconditions: User can navigate to Stories and Help & Resources pages.
steps: Navigate to the Help & Resources page (/help-center).
expectedResults: The 'Catalog' link is visible in the navigation bar on the Stories page.
The 'Catalog' link is visible in the navigation bar on the Help & Resources page.

testCases.title: Navigation Bar Highlights Active Catalog Tab
testCases.preconditions: User is on the catalog page.
steps: Inspect the 'Catalog' link for active/current class or aria-current.
expectedResults: The 'Catalog' link has an active/current visual indicator or aria-current attribute.

testCases.title: Catalog Link href is Correct
testCases.preconditions: User is on any page with the navigation bar visible.
expectedResults: The 'Catalog' link href attribute contains '/catalog'.

testCases.title: Product Cards are Visible on Catalog Landing Page
testCases.preconditions: User is on the catalog page with products available.
steps: Verify at least one product card with a heading is visible.
Verify at least one 'View details' link is present.
expectedResults: At least one product card with a heading is visible.
At least one 'View details' link is present on a product card.

testCases.title: Catalog Page Has Correct Document Title
testCases.preconditions: User is on the catalog page.
expectedResults: document.title equals 'Assistive Device Catalog – AT/AD Portal'.

testCases.title: Navigation Bar Remains Visible on Catalog Page
testCases.preconditions: User has navigated to the catalog page.
steps: Verify 'Home', 'Catalog', and 'Stories' links are present in the header.
expectedResults: The navigation bar is visible on the catalog page.
'Home', 'Catalog', and 'Stories' links are present in the header.

testCases.title: Tablet Viewport Shows Catalog Navigation Correctly
testCases.preconditions: User is on the homepage at tablet viewport (768x1024).
steps: Navigate to the homepage at tablet viewport.
Verify navigation to the catalog page and H1 heading.
expectedResults: The 'Catalog' link is accessible (directly or via hamburger menu).
Clicking 'Catalog' navigates to the catalog page.
The H1 heading 'Assistive Device Catalog' is visible.

testCases.title: Back Button Returns to Homepage from Catalog
testCases.preconditions: User is on the homepage.
expectedResults: The catalog page loads after clicking the link.
Clicking back returns the user to the homepage.
The homepage URL and content are correct.

testCases.title: Catalog Page Footer is Visible
testCases.preconditions: User is on the catalog page.
steps: Scroll to the bottom of the page.
Verify a footer element is visible.
expectedResults: A footer element is visible at the bottom of the catalog page.

testCases.title: Rapid Double-Click on Catalog Link Navigates Correctly
testCases.preconditions: User is on the homepage.
steps: Verify the catalog page is displayed correctly.
expectedResults: The catalog page loads correctly despite the double-click.
The H1 heading and URL are correct.

testCases.title: Catalog Page URL is Canonical (No Double Slashes)
testCases.preconditions: User has navigated to the catalog page.
steps: Inspect the URL path after the domain.
Verify no double slashes exist in the path.
expectedResults: The URL path does not contain double slashes (e.g., no '//catalog').

testCases.title: Forward Button Works After Back from Catalog
testCases.preconditions: User is on the homepage.
expectedResults: Back button returns to the homepage.
Forward button navigates back to the catalog page.
The catalog page URL and H1 heading are correct.

testCases.title: Page Scroll Position Resets on Catalog Navigation
testCases.preconditions: User is on the homepage.
steps: Check the scroll position on the catalog page.
expectedResults: The catalog page loads with scroll position at the top (0 or near 0).

testCases.title: Catalog Page Has No Console Errors on Load
testCases.preconditions: Browser console is open/monitored.
steps: Wait for page to fully load.
expectedResults: No JavaScript console errors are logged during page load.

testCases.title: Multiple Rapid Navigations Home→Catalog→Home→Catalog
testCases.preconditions: User is on the homepage.
steps: Click 'Catalog' link again — verify catalog page.
expectedResults: Each navigation to the catalog page loads correctly.
The catalog page displays the correct H1 heading and URL after each navigation.

testCases.title: Catalog Link Visible After Scrolling Down on Homepage
testCases.preconditions: User is on the homepage.
steps: Check if the 'Catalog' link is still visible (sticky nav).
If not visible, scroll back to top and verify.
expectedResults: The 'Catalog' link remains visible via sticky navigation, or is accessible by scrolling to top.

testCases.title: Direct URL Access to Catalog Works Without Homepage
testCases.preconditions: No prior navigation; fresh browser session.
steps: Navigate directly to the catalog URL.
Verify H1 heading, device count, and pagination are visible.
expectedResults: The catalog page loads successfully via direct URL.
H1 heading, device count, and pagination are all visible.

testCases.title: No Horizontal Overflow on Mobile After Catalog Navigation
testCases.preconditions: User is on the homepage at mobile viewport (375x667).
steps: Navigate to the homepage at mobile viewport.
Open hamburger menu and click 'Catalog' link.
Verify no horizontal overflow on the catalog page.
expectedResults: The catalog page has no horizontal scrollbar at mobile viewport.
document.body.scrollWidth does not exceed viewport width.
